Fix reasoning style detection: use 'reasoning' param not 'reasoning_effort'

All OpenRouter reasoning models (GPT-5.4, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek) expose
effort control via the 'reasoning' object param, not 'reasoning_effort'.
Only ~2 gpt-oss models have 'reasoning_effort' as a standalone param.

Fix: getReasoningStyle now returns 'effort' when model has 'reasoning' in
supported_parameters, showing Max/High/Medium/Low/Minimal buttons for all
reasoning-capable models.

commit.message:
Replace raw PPG waveform with synthetic ECG triggered by beat detection

The MAX30102 is a PPG sensor — its raw IR signal is a smooth sinusoidal
wave (blood volume changes), not the sharp QRS spikes of an ECG. No
amount of filtering can make it look like a hospital monitor.

New approach: define a 28-sample PQRST waveform template in PROGMEM
with the classic ECG shape (P wave, QRS spike, T wave). checkForBeat()
triggers playback of the template into the scrolling buffer. Between
beats the line stays flat at the baseline.

Removes all DC removal / EMA / AC scaling / calibration warmup code —
none of it is needed since we're no longer displaying the raw signal.

fix: 重构置闰逻辑 - 基于符头到芒种/大雪距离判断

核心流程：查节气 → 判超神 → 做置闰 → 定局数

置闰判断：
1. 找到最近的芒种或大雪（前后都找）
2. 计算符头到芒种/大雪的天数
3. 如果距离 > 9天：
   - 在芒种/大雪之后 > 9天：继续用芒种/大雪（闰局）
   - 在芒种/大雪之前 > 9天：提前用芒种/大雪（闰局）

Implement Phase 5.4: Damage Tracking

Add damage tracking infrastructure to optimize rendering by only
re-rendering changed regions using GPU scissor rects.

Changes:
- Created damage.go with DamageTracker for tracking dirty regions
- Implemented region coalescing to merge overlapping/adjacent areas
- Added FilterCommandsByDamage to filter commands by intersection
- Created scissor.go with GPU scissor rect encoding
- Integrated scissor state into batch submission pipeline
- Added RenderWithDamage() API to backend
- 29 tests added (all passing)

Implementation details:
- DamageTracker supports add, clear, coalesce, and bounds operations
- ComputeDamageForCommand calculates damage rect for each command type
- ScissorRect encoding follows Intel Gen9+ 3DSTATE_SCISSOR format
- ClampScissorRect ensures rects stay within render target bounds
- Zero regression in complexity metrics (20 improvements, 2 minor increases)

Phase 5.4 complete. Ready for Phase 5.5 (full demo app).

commit.message:
feat: /health endpoint with real diagnostic checks and 503 on failure (#99)

The existing `/api/health` stub returned a hardcoded `{ status:
"healthy" }` with no actual infrastructure checks. This replaces it with
a proper diagnostic endpoint suitable for post-deployment smoke tests
and monitoring.

## Changes

### `api/src/services/health-checks.js` (new)
Four named checks with a `runAllChecks(options)` aggregator:

| Check | What it tests |
|---|---|
| `storage_connection_string` | `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` env
var is set |
| `table_storage` | Live `TableServiceClient.listTables()` connectivity
probe |
| `scenarios_table` | Reports active table name (`TABLE_NAME_SCENARIOS`
or `userscenarios`) |
| `profiles_table` | Reports active table name (`TABLE_NAME_PROFILES` or

commit.message:
Fix continuous wave: switch to unipolar pulse display

The bipolar AC scaling (tracking both acMin and acMax) stretched the
dip between beats all the way to the top of the display, making it look
like a continuous sine wave with no gaps between beats.

Switch to unipolar display matching real pulse oximeters:
- Only track positive peaks (acMax) with slow decay (0.998/sample)
- Map [0, acMax] → [bottom, top]; negative values clamp to bottom
- Result: flat baseline between beats, sharp spike up on each heartbeat

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix symmetric triangle waveform: slow EMA alpha 0.05 -> 0.005

With alpha=0.05 the DC baseline had a ~0.4s time constant — shorter than
the ~0.87s beat interval at 69 BPM — so the EMA was tracking the pulse
itself, producing a symmetric up/down triangle with no gaps.

Changing to alpha=0.005 gives a ~4s time constant. The baseline now tracks
only slow drift, leaving the true PPG pulse shape (fast rise, slower fall
with dicrotic notch, flat gap between beats) visible in the AC signal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: 修正置闰逻辑 - 超神方向

置闰条件：符头到下一个芒种/大雪的超神天数>9
- 超神 = 符头在节气之前
- 找下一个芒种/大雪节气

Fix 1-pixel waveform: replace abs scaling with DC removal

The previous approach scaled the full absolute IR range (e.g. 240k-260k),
but the actual pulse variation is only ~1000-3000 units — a tiny fraction
that rendered as 1-2 pixels.

Replace with DC removal:
- Slow EMA (alpha=0.05) tracks the large constant baseline (DC level)
- Subtract DC to isolate only the pulse variation (AC component)
- Auto-scale the AC min/max (with slow decay) to fill the display height
- Pulse waveform now fills the full 38px waveform area

commit.message:
fix: reliably read session in tRPC context using next/headers cookies()

Root cause: auth() uses createActionURL() which, without AUTH_URL set, falls
back to x-forwarded-proto header. In local dev this header is absent, so it
defaults to 'https' → useSecureCookies=true → looks for '__Secure-authjs.session-token'
cookie. But the login set an unscoped 'authjs.session-token' cookie (HTTP).
Cookie name/salt mismatch → JWE decryption fails → session null → UNAUTHORIZED.

Fix:
- Use cookies() from next/headers in createContext — guaranteed correct in
  Next.js route handlers regardless of request headers
- Try both cookie names (HTTP and HTTPS) so the same code works in dev and prod
- Add AUTH_URL=http://localhost:3000 to .env.local as the canonical base URL

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix waveform stuck at top: correct auto-scale initial values

irMin was initialized to 200000 but real finger IR readings are often
245000+, so irMin never got updated and the artificial floor compressed
all real signal variation to the very top of the display.

Initialize irMin=300000 (will drop on first reading) and irMax=0
(will rise on first reading) so auto-scaling tracks the actual signal.
